title: processLayout
text: processLayout A function that informs a widget to provide a processed initial HTML layout to be used at a run-time. static processLayout(skeletonWidgetElement, objectDefinition) Arguments skeletonWidgetElement Specifies a reference to the root element of the skeleton layout. objectDefinition Gives access to the objectDefinition of the object (field, occurrence, collection, or component) that the widget will be used for. Return value processLayout function returns the HTML layout, and its root element should be HTMLElement . Exceptions If processLayout root element is not HTMLElement , the framework throws an exception, for example: Explain this code Function processLayout of acme.mywidget does not return an HTMLElement. Copy Where, acme.mywidget is the name of the UX widget class. Description Uniface calls the processLayout() function to inform the widget class to provide the processed layout to be used at run-time by widgets of the UX widget class. The skeletonWidgetElement argument passed to this function is part of the declared DSP layout that is bound to the field, occurrence, collection, or component. By default, this layout is defined using the Design Layout worksheet in the DSP Component Editor in the IDE, but it could also be generated using code either on the server or the client. The processLayout() function of all widgets are fired in the order of the elements in the DSP's Design Layout. This means that the processLayout() function of a parent object can generate or influence the skeleton layout of its child objects. For example, an entity widget generates the skeleton layout of its fields. The processLayout() function must return a reference to the bound element of the new or updated widget layout. With objectDefinition as the second parameter of the processLayout function of the UX widget class, you can get access to objectDefinition of the object (field, occurrence, collection, or component) that the widget class is for. If the object is an occurrence, a collection, or a component, the objectDefinition parameter also gives access to the definitions of child objects, like fields or inner entities. The processLayout function can make changes to the objectDefinition parameter and its child definitions. With this, the processLayout function can influence the behavior of subsequent processLayout calls for child objects. The objectDefinition parameter typically gives access to properties that were defined for the object in the IDE's Property Inspector. The objectDefinition contains the following interfaces: getName() : Returns the fully qualified name of the object, such as MYFIELD.MYENT.MYMODEL for a field, or MYENT.MYMODEL for an entity. getShortName() : Returns the unqualified name of the object, such as MYFIELD for a field or MYENT for an entity. getType() : Returns the objectDefinition type. Note: At definition level, the concept of occurrence and collection do not exist: field for a field-level definition. entity for an entity-level definition. component for a component-level definition. getWidgetClass() : Returns the name of the field-level widget class, such as specified by the widget_class property of the physical widget definition in web.ini . This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is field . setWidgetClass(widgetClass) : Changes the field-level widget class. This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is field . getOccurrenceWidgetClass() : Returns the name of the occurrence widget class as specified by the occurrence_widget_class property of the physical widget definition in web.ini . This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is entity . setOccurrenceWidgetClass(occurrenceWidgetClass) : Changes the occurrence widget class. This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is entity . getCollectionWidgetClass() : Returns the name of the collection widget class as specified by the collection_widget_class property of the physical widget definition in web.ini . This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is entity . setCollectionWidgetClass(collectionwidgetclass) : Changes the collection widget class. This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is entity . getComponentWidgetClass() : Returns the name of the component widget class as specified by the component_widget_class property of the physical widget definition in web.ini . This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is component . setComponentWidgetClass(componentWidgetClass) : Changes the component widget class. This function is available only if the objectDefinition type is component . getPropertyNames() : Returns an array containing the names of all define-time properties of the object. getProperty(propertyName) : Returns the value of the indicated define-time property. setProperty(propertyName, value) : Overrides the value of the indicated define-time property or adds the property as a new define-time property. getChildDefinitions() : Returns an array of the objectDefinition of the children of the current objectDefinition . setOccurrenceProperties(properties) : Sets declarative occurrence‑level properties for occurrence widgets. This function is available only when the objectDefinition type is entity . Note: Widget developers can define both collection‑level and occurrence‑level properties in the same entity property definition field in the IDE Property Inspector ( DSP Widget Properties ), as long as they can be differentiated. To view code snippet for moving declarative occurrence‑level properties from the entity object definition within the collection widget’s processLayout API function, see Example: UX Widget processLayout() . Note: At definition-level, the concept of occurrence and collection do not exist. Compare this to the component structure, where only fields and entities exist. Hence, the objectDefinition object for type entity has separate properties for the occurrence-level and collection-level widget classes. objectDefinition Interface Reference The objectDefinition parameter provides access to the widget's definition properties and child definitions. The available methods depend on the widget type. Table 1. objectDefinition Methods by Widget Type Method Field Entity Component Access Level Returns Description getName() Read-only String Fully qualified name (e.g., "MYFIELD.MYENT.MYMODEL") getShortName() Read-only String Unqualified name (e.g., "MYFIELD" or "MYENT") getType() Read-only String Returns "field", "entity", or "component" getPropertyNames() Read-only Array<String> Array of all define-time property names getProperty(name) Read-only String Value of the specified define-time property setProperty(name, value) processLayout only void Sets or overrides a define-time property getWidgetClass() Read-only String Field widget class name from web.ini setWidgetClass(class) processLayout only void Changes the field widget class getOccurrenceWidgetClass() Read-only String Occurrence widget class name from web.ini setOccurrenceWidgetClass(class) processLayout only void Changes the occurrence widget class getCollectionWidgetClass() Read-only String Collection widget class name from web.ini setCollectionWidgetClass(class) processLayout only void Changes the collection widget class getComponentWidgetClass() Read-only String Component widget class name from web.ini setComponentWidgetClass(class) processLayout only void Changes the component widget class getChildDefinitions() Read-only Array<objectDefinition> Array of child object definitions setOccurrenceProperties(properties) processLayout only void Sets declarative occurrence‑level properties for occurrence widgets. Legend : = Method is available for this widget type = Method is only available in processLayout() function = Method is not available for this widget type Related Links onConnect Initializes the current widget when it is connected to a Document Object Model (DOM) element. dataUpdate Updates a widget's state when the data object's data is changed.
